settings.title: A Spring Workshop for Local Makers
settings.excerpt: One evening, a small room, and the brand basics every small business should know.
settings.body:
We get a lot of questions from local makers who cannot justify a full design budget yet but want to look the part.

So we are running a free evening workshop in March covering the essentials: choosing typefaces that work together, building a colour palette that survives real life, and laying out a flyer that people actually read.

What the evening covers

We are keeping it practical rather than theoretical. Everything we cover is something you can do that same week with the tools you already have, no expensive software and no design degree required.

  • Pairing two typefaces that work without fighting each other.
  • Building a small palette that still looks right in print and on a phone.
  • Laying out a flyer so the eye lands where you want it.

It is hands on and capped at twelve people so everyone gets time at the table. Bring a project you are stuck on, and we will work through it together.

Who it is for

This is aimed at people running a stall, a side business, or a small shop who are doing their own design out of necessity. You do not need any background, just something real you are trying to make look better.

Twelve seats go quickly, so let us know soon if you would like one. Come with the half-finished poster or the logo you are unsure about, and you will leave with it in better shape than you arrived.
